source_title: Scott Galloway: Why I'm selling my American stocks

paragraphs.speaker: sam
paragraphs.name: Sam Parr
paragraphs.text: We have a little schtick for you today. We want to ask you the 10 spiciest burning questions we had. You did this post the other day which was pretty hilarious. You said, I finally know what it feels like to be an attractive woman, because I'm at Davos and everyone's looking down at my chest and deciding if they're going to talk to me or not. When you're around all these incredibly successful people, who had the most gravity towards them?

paragraphs.speaker: scott
paragraphs.name: Scott Galloway
paragraphs.text: Oh, hands down, Governor Newsom. I walked down into the main area of the Congress Hall. Gavin Newsom was walking around like I'm the president. And everyone believed him. That guy has such star power. He spots me in the corner of his eye. And he comes over to me. And he hugs me. And he says, keep doing what you're doing. Governor Newsom has that star power. Hands down.

paragraphs.speaker: shaan
paragraphs.name: Shaan Puri
paragraphs.text: You're a grown man. You've got $100 million. You've got fame. You've got family. You've got influence. What is it that you're still hungry for that money has not been able to buy so far?

paragraphs.speaker: scott
paragraphs.name: Scott Galloway
paragraphs.text: I want to launch good men. I feel like I have a deficit. I think I've taken more from the country and from taxpayers and from the freedoms I've enjoyed than I've given back. So I want to get to a point of surplus value. And my purpose is I want to raise good men. I want to know that when I'm towards the end that I've raised really confident, loving men. My purpose the first 40 years of my life was I wanted to be really rich and really awesome so I could take care of my mom and be more attractive to strange women.

paragraphs.speaker: scott
paragraphs.name: Scott Galloway
paragraphs.text: The big pivot for me in my life, hands down, was when my mom got really sick and I couldn't take care of her. That just sort of changed everything for me. Money is a means of protecting people, not of having an awesome life. The death of my mother and the birth of my boys. It was 2008. I'd lost everything. And I just felt this shame. I'm like, I've been so successful. I've had so many blessings. And I'm near broke. And now I'm responsible for this little science experiment.

paragraphs.speaker: scott
paragraphs.name: Scott Galloway
paragraphs.text: From 25 to 45, maybe 48, I did nothing but work. I don't remember anything from the age of 25 to 45 other than working. If I hit this number, I'm done. Whenever I meet with my financial advisors and I do this once a year, they tell me, this is your net worth. And if it's above that number, I do one of two things. I either spend it. I am so good at spending money. I spend money like a 50s gangster just diagnosed with ass cancer. I am living large, and I love it. Or I give it away. I do it because it makes me feel masculine and American.

paragraphs.speaker: scott
paragraphs.name: Scott Galloway
paragraphs.text: Hands down, a private plane. Hands down. It lowers the bar for fun. Fractional is the way to go because you essentially can arbitrage, scale up or down based on the mission. I spend about $1.5 or $2 million a year, but a friend of mine from high school I really like, a Mormon kid getting married in a weird place. I'm going, why? Because I have a private plane. I can spend another 17 days a year, either with my family or doing something fun because I'm not connecting through Dallas Fort Worth and getting delayed. Take my house, don't take my plane.

paragraphs.speaker: scott
paragraphs.name: Scott Galloway
paragraphs.text: I'm selling down my American stocks, mostly because I'm diversifying. I've been rich three times, which means I've lost it twice. I can't go back. The Buffett Index, stock market valuation versus GDP, he likes it at about 90%. It's at 210%. The S&P is trading at historic highs. 97% of the time, the S&P has traded at lower valuations relative to earnings. Every public company in America, every public company outside of America, they're equally priced right now. I think America's too expensive right now. I think Europe's on sale.

paragraphs.speaker: scott
paragraphs.name: Scott Galloway
paragraphs.text: GLP-1 is the most transformative technology of the last 20 or 30 years. It's the biggest thing since GPS.
